2605.30265.title: LoMo: Local Modality Substitution for Deeper Vision-Language Fusion
2605.30265.paper_summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved substantial progress across a wide range of understanding and reasoning tasks, driven by large-scale image-text training aimed at multimodal fusion. Ideally, replacing a textual question with its rendered-image counterpart should leave model performance essentially unaffected. In practice, however, such modality substitution induces dramatic performance degradation. We attribute this "carrier sensitivity" issue to an inherent bias in current training corpora. Across prevalent datasets such as image captioning, VQA, OCR, and web-sourced interleaved data, text and images are typically organized into distinct and asymmetric roles, with text serving as linguistic queries and images as visual references. Such data bias leads VLMs to exhibit distinct preferences for information acquisition across different modalities. Consequently, VLMs fail to align representations of semantically equivalent content across textual and visual carriers, making model reasoning fragile under modality substitution. To address this, we propose Local Modality Substitution (LoMo), a lightweight, architecture-agnostic data curation paradigm designed to provide supervision for cross-modal representational invariance between semantically equivalent text and image carriers. LoMo achieves this by reformulating single-modality prompts into seamlessly interleaved multimodal sequences. It dynamically selects target text spans and recasts them as rendered images, thereby preserving the same semantics across "text, visual, text" carriers. Extensive experiments across 13 diverse multimodal benchmarks demonstrate that LoMo significantly improves overall multimodal reasoning and yields deeper cross-modal fusion. Specifically, it delivers consistent gains across foundational models, improving over standard SFT by 2.67 points on LLaVA-OneVision-1.5-8B and 2.82 points on Qwen3.5-9B.
2605.30265.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在理解和推理任務方面取得了顯著進展，這得益於旨在多模態融合的大規模圖像-文本訓練。理想情況下，將文本問題替換為其渲染圖像對應物應該不會對模型性能產生實質影響。然而，在實踐中，這種模態替換會導致性能劇烈下降。我們將這種「載體敏感性」問題歸因於當前訓練語料中的固有偏見。在圖像標註、VQA、OCR 和網絡來源的交錯數據等常見數據集上，文本和圖像通常被組織成不同且不對稱的角色，文本作為語言查詢，圖像作為視覺參考。這種數據偏見導致 VLMs 在不同模態之間的信息獲取上表現出明顯的偏好。因此，VLMs 無法對齊文本和視覺載體之間語義等價內容的表示，使得模型推理在模態替換下變得脆弱。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了本地模態替換（LoMo），這是一種輕量級、架構無關的數據策劃範式，旨在為語義等價的文本和圖像載體之間的跨模態表示不變性提供監督。LoMo 通過將單模態提示重新格式化為無縫交錯的多模態序列來實現這一點。它動態選擇目標文本範圍並將其重新表達為渲染圖像，從而在「文本、視覺、文本」載體之間保留相同的語義。在 13 個多樣化的多模態基準上進行的廣泛實驗表明，LoMo 顯著改善了整體多模態推理並實現了更深層次的跨模態融合。具體而言，它在基礎模型上提供了一致的增益，LLaVA-OneVision-1.5-8B 上提高了 2.67 分，Qwen3.5-9B 上提高了 2.82 分。
2605.30265.author: Feng Han et.al.
2605.30265.authors: Feng Han, Zhixiong Zhang, Zheming Liang, Yibin Wang, Jiaqi Wang
